note: Catalog entries are NOT deep-researched records. They exist so every job is findable. Pay/retirement/transition/crosswalk deliberately absent rather than invented. This list is deliberately short: the Space Force is a small branch and its enlisted specialty list is genuinely narrow. Every code field is 'UNVERIFIED' — no numeric Space Force Specialty Code (SFSC) could be confirmed against a primary .mil source this session (the official spaceforce.mil SFSC crosswalk PDF and the CFETP 1C6X1 PDF both returned HTTP 403). As of retrieval, the live spaceforce.com career pages (/careers/, /enlisted-careers/, /career-finder/, /education/enlisted/, /how-to-join/enlisted-guardians/) all serve a 'Maintenance Page' placeholder (HTTP 200, ~1.3KB, verified 2026-07-12) rather than real content. Data below was recovered from Wayback Machine (web.archive.org) snapshots of the same official spaceforce.com domain, dated 2026-06-22 and 2026-07-10 — the most recent available captures of the real page content, not a guessed or fabricated substitute.

specialties.name: All Source Analyst
specialties.what_it_is: All Source Analysts discover, develop, and assess raw data from multiple sources to produce intelligence supporting Space Force operations.

specialties.name: Cryptologic Analyst
specialties.what_it_is: Cryptologic Analysts identify, analyze, and report on cyberspace-based threats to support intelligence operations.

specialties.name: Cyber Operations
specialties.what_it_is: Cyber Operations specialists protect Space Force networks and systems, covering defense, radio frequency and satellite communications, systems, and network infrastructure.

specialties.name: Geospatial Intelligence Analyst
specialties.what_it_is: Geospatial Intelligence Analysts analyze satellite imagery to identify activity and assess threats.

specialties.name: Signals Intelligence Analyst
specialties.what_it_is: Signals Intelligence Analysts extract and analyze foreign communications through electromagnetic emissions.

specialties.name: Space Systems Operator
specialties.what_it_is: Space Systems Operators detect and track satellites and missile launches, operate space control systems, and support military satellite launch and on-orbit operations.

specialties.name: Targeting Analyst
specialties.what_it_is: Targeting Analysts develop intelligence to analyze targets and assess threats.

sources.title: Discover Space Force Careers | U.S. Space Force — Wayback Machine archive (used because live spaceforce.com/careers currently serves a maintenance placeholder)
Space Systems Operator - Enlisted Careers - U.S. Space Force — Wayback Machine archive of individual specialty detail page
Live spaceforce.com/careers/ (confirmed HTTP 200 but serving 'Maintenance Page' placeholder content, not real career data)
spaceforce.mil root and Fact Sheets index (confirmed HTTP 403, unreachable, as anticipated by task brief)
CFETP 1C6X1 (Space Systems Operations career field training plan) — official Air Force e-publishing PDF, confirmed HTTP 403

Space Force space Force Specialty Codes | MOS Roadmap — independent secondary aggregator, used only as corroboration that the same 7 names carry an 'Enlisted' tag (not used as the basis for the list itself)

Space Force Specialty Codes — Veteran.com (dated March 28, 2022; used only to document the existence of legacy pre-renumbering AFSC-style codes, explicitly NOT used as a source of current SFSC codes — see unverified list)

unverified: Every numeric SFSC code: could not be confirmed against any live primary .mil source this session. spaceforce.mil returned HTTP 403 site-wide; the official 'ENLISTED ONLY USSF SPECIALTY CODE CROSSWALK' PDF (https://www.spaceforce.mil/LinkClick.aspx?fileticket=csSHRIrRU8c%3D) also returned HTTP 403; the CFETP 1C6X1 PDF at static.e-publishing.af.mil returned HTTP 403. Per the binding constraint, every code field is set to the literal string 'UNVERIFIED' rather than a guessed number.

A dated (March 28, 2022) secondary source, veteran.com, lists legacy AFSC-style codes (1C6 Space Systems Operations, 1N0 All Source Intelligence, 1N1 Geospatial Intelligence, 1N2 Signals Intelligence, 3D0/3D1 Cyber Operations/Support) and explicitly states the Space Force 'currently using the AFSC structure' and that 'this list... continues to grow and has not been finalized.' Because this source pre-dates the SFSC renumbering reported to have occurred since 2020, and does not itself claim these are current confirmed SFSCs, these legacy codes were deliberately NOT used to fill the code field — doing so would misrepresent a stale, hedged secondary source as a verified current code.

Whether these 7 specialties are the complete and final universe of ALL current Space Force enlisted specialties (versus, e.g., additional narrow specialties for prior-service Air Force transfers not surfaced on the public recruiting site) cannot be ruled out with certainty. Confidence is high because two independent sources converge on the same 7 names: the official spaceforce.com site's own 'filter by specialty > Enlisted' tag (Wayback archive, 2026-06-22) and the independent aggregator mosroadmap.com (live, 2026-07-12), which separately tags the same 7 roles 'Enlisted' versus 'Officer'/'Civilian' for its ~30 other listed specialties.

Clearance level per specialty: the Space Systems Operator detail page (archived 2026-07-10) lists 'Successful completion of a Single Scope Background Investigation (SSBI)' as a qualification, which is typically associated with Top Secret/SCI-tier access — but this single data point was not extended to the other 6 specialties (whose individual detail pages were not fetched this session), so clearance is null for all 7 rather than inferred.

ASVAB minimum score per specialty: the Space Systems Operator detail page states 'Obtain a qualifying ASVAB score: electronics minimum score of 60' (implying a MAGE-Electronics composite), but again this single data point was not extended to the other 6 specialties without individual confirmation, so asvab is null for all 7 for consistency rather than partially filled in.
completion_accountability: 7 of 7 Space Force enlisted specialties catalogued: All Source Analyst, Cryptologic Analyst, Cyber Operations, Geospatial Intelligence Analyst, Signals Intelligence Analyst, Space Systems Operator, Targeting Analyst. Every entry mapped to a valid interest-taxonomy.json cluster id. This list is intentionally short and was NOT padded — Acquisition Manager, seen adjacent to these on the same archived page, was explicitly excluded because it is tagged Officer, not Enlisted, on the official site (corroborated by mosroadmap.com's 'Acquisition Manager Officer' tag). Every code field is the literal string 'UNVERIFIED' per the binding constraint; no numeric SFSC was fabricated. Source of the list itself is the official spaceforce.com domain, recovered via Wayback Machine archive because the live career pages currently return a maintenance placeholder (confirmed by direct fetch, 2026-07-12) rather than because spaceforce.com was abandoned as a source. Web calls used this session for the Space Force portion: roughly 20, spent mostly ruling out dead ends (spaceforce.com/careers/, /enlisted-careers/, /career-finder/, /education/enlisted/, /how-to-join/enlisted-guardians/ all serving the maintenance placeholder; spaceforce.mil and its PDFs returning 403) before the Wayback Machine approach succeeded.
